pages.text: Nailiana elegans comes from the middle Cambrian, with fossils found in the Maotianshan Shales, South China. that had 8 tentacles surrounding a mouth and oral disc, and grew up to 6 cm in height. Nailiana represents not only an early stem-cnidarian from the Cambrian, but also represents the earliest known macrophagous predator in the fossil record (meaning it went after large prey).

The generic name derives from the forename of Nailian Fu, who was one of the researchers grandmother, as well as also meaning 'lotus-like' in Chinese. The specific name derives from the Latin word 'elegans', to mean 'elegant'.
